segments.text: There we sat down, yea, we wept When we remembered Zion.
We hung our harps Upon the willows in the midst of it.
For there those who carried us away captive asked of us a song, And those who plundered us requested mirth,
Sing us one of the songs of Zion!
segments.speaker: Captors

segments.text: How shall we sing the Lord's song In a foreign land?
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

segments.text: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, Let my right hand forget its skill!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: If I do not remember you, Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth— If I do not exalt Jerusalem Above my chief joy.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Remember, O Lord, against the sons of Edom The day of Jerusalem,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Raze it, raze it, To its very foundation!
segments.speaker: Sons of Edom

segments.text: O daughter of Babylon, who are to be destroyed, Happy the one who repays you as you have served us!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Happy the one who takes and dashes Your little ones against the rock!
segments.speaker: Psalmist
